authoritative_sources.title: Overview of U.S. Requirements for Textiles and Apparel
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's apparel-specific overview is official named-industry guidance describing the federal product-safety requirements, testing, and certification posture for textiles and apparel sold in the United States.

authoritative_sources.title: Flammable Fabrics Act (FFA)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's FFA guidance is the authoritative public source for mandatory flammability standards affecting clothing textiles and children's sleepwear.

authoritative_sources.title: Drawstrings in Children's Upper Outerwear
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's drawstring guidance is the authoritative public apparel-safety reference for a specific children's outerwear hazard that materially changes shipment and escalation decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Threading Your Way Through the Labeling Requirements Under the Textile and Wool Acts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's guide is the official workflow reference for textile labeling evidence including fiber content, country of origin, and dealer identity.

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's care-label guide is the official public source for permanent attachment, legibility, and reasonable-basis expectations for garment care instructions.
used_for: release-boundary handling for missing care support

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 2859-1:2026 - Sampling procedures for inspection by attributes — Part 1: Sampling schemes indexed by acceptance quality limit (AQL) for lot-by-lot inspection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO's official AQL standard is a recognized inspection-control reference for lot-by-lot acceptance and rejection decisions that structure apparel final-random-inspection practice.

authoritative_sources.title: AATCC TM8 - Test Method for Colorfastness to Crocking: Crockmeter
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AATCC's official TM8 page is a recognized textile-quality evidence standard for evaluating color transfer defects in apparel and textile goods.

authoritative_sources.title: AATCC TM135 - Test Method for Dimensional Changes of Fabrics after Home Laundering
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AATCC's official TM135 page is a recognized textile-quality evidence standard for shrinkage and dimensional-stability claims used in apparel QA review.

authoritative_sources.title: ASTM D123 - Standard Terminology Relating to Textiles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTM's textile terminology standard is a recognized public reference for normalizing the record language used in QA findings and test summaries.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 17708:2018 - Footwear — Test methods for whole shoe — Upper sole adhesion
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO's official upper-sole adhesion method is a recognized footwear-quality evidence standard for bonding failures and production-control ageing checks.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 24266:2020 - Footwear — Test methods for whole shoe — Flexing durability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO's official whole-shoe flexing method is a recognized footwear-quality evidence standard for durability failure review and comparative shipment readiness analysis.

workflow_stages.name: pre-production quality baseline review
workflow_stages.description: Review tech packs, graded specs, approved samples, workmanship standards, and label support to determine what evidence must exist before inline and final production quality decisions are credible.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: inline and final inspection disposition
workflow_stages.description: Assess inline, endline, and final-random-inspection results against tenant AQL and customer rules to decide whether visible evidence supports hold, release recommendation, or escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: lab evidence and labeling review
workflow_stages.description: Review flammability, crocking, dimensional stability, care-label support, and textile-label evidence to determine whether shipment or closure is supportable.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: footwear durability and bonding review
workflow_stages.description: Review whole-shoe flexing and upper-sole adhesion evidence when footwear performance defects affect shipment release, hold scope, or CAPA packaging.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: defect, incident, and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold and escalation posture when repeat defects, children's-product hazards, or distributed-product incidents require formal safety, legal, technical, or executive review.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: tech packs, BOM and trim sheets, graded measurement tables, workmanship standards, and fit or sample comments
PP sample approvals, TOP sample approvals, inline and final-random-inspection reports, AQL plans, and defect photos
lab-test reports for flammability, crocking, dimensional stability, footwear adhesion, footwear flexing, and care-label support
fiber-content, country-of-origin, care-label, tracking-label, GCC or CPC support records
shipment holds, nonconformance reports, CAPA records, vendor corrective-action responses, and release-request summaries
complaint, returns, and incident summaries tied to style, SKU, lot, vendor, or distributed-product scope

allowed: assess whether visible apparel, textile, or footwear evidence supports hold, bounded release recommendation, investigation, corrective-action escalation, or findings packaging
organize missing records, failed test evidence, open inspection defects, and required next owners
summarize labeling, sample, inspection, and performance evidence conflicts without overstating authority
escalate: final legal or regulatory interpretation, final GCC or CPC issuance, or regulator-facing communication
technical-design, pattern, construction, grade-rule, or fit approval that changes the product definition
vendor substitution, sourcing approval, commercial exception approval, or executive shipment override
product-safety incidents involving distributed goods, consumer hazard posture, or recall command
refuse: override documented hold, drawstring, flammability, labeling, or inspection controls
treat incomplete or failed inspection, test, or label evidence as acceptable proof of shipment readiness or closure
approve technical or commercial changes where formal quality, technical-design, compliance, or sourcing approval is still required

domain_failure_modes: goods are cleared without approved sample, measurement, inspection, or test evidence tied to the actual style and SKU
a children's-product or flammability hazard is treated as a routine quality defect instead of a safety-triggered escalation
failed crocking, dimensional-stability, adhesion, or flexing evidence is normalized away because launch timing is under pressure
the lane drifts into technical-design approval, vendor substitution approval, legal labeling interpretation, or executive shipment override
mixed product category, age-grade, or jurisdiction facts lead the lane to apply the wrong apparel or footwear baseline

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-category variability across general apparel, children's products, sleepwear, textiles, and footwear
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats product category, age grade, and jurisdiction as mandatory tenant facts and refuses to generalize one product-safety rule across the entire industry.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer manuals, restricted-substance rules, and jurisdiction-specific obligations beyond the public U.S. baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack preserves the public baseline but requires runtime retrieval of customer and jurisdiction overlays and routes legal or vendor-compliance interpretation when those overlays change the answer.

notes: The public baseline supports apparel, textile, and footwear quality evidence handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact product category, customer manuals, age-grade scope, shipment authority, and system-of-record connectors.
The specialist is intentionally bounded to quality-assurance evidence review and must not imply final technical-design, legal, vendor-compliance, or executive release authority.
